codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly satisfies all validation assertions (VAL-CTX-001 through VAL-CTX-005). The feature was primarily test-driven: the worker discovered that the existing server.js implementation already correctly implements stateless context preservation. No changes to server.js were needed. All 14 new tests pass, confirming proper handling of questionText verbatim usage, screenshotImage inclusion in LLM calls, validation of refinement requests, and complete request isolation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the TDD approach specified in backend-worker skill: wrote failing tests first, discovered existing implementation already satisfied requirements, then verified all tests pass. No deviations from procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker created tests first (context-preservation.test.js), ran them to verify they pass, then did manual verification. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true confirms this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used the standard test server pattern (port 3001 for tests, port 3000 for main server) as documented in AGENTS.md. No new services were introduced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Test file uses TEST_PORT = 3001, server started on port 3000 for manual curl tests. No changes to .factory/services.yaml needed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that the API was already stateless - no session state exists in server.js. The callLLM function (line ~451-480) uses the provided 'text' and 'image' parameters verbatim in LLM calls, with no state preservation between requests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker examined server.js lines 451-480 and confirmed: 'The callLLM function uses text parameter (which is questionText) verbatim in the LLM call (line 460) and includes image parameter (which is screenshotImage) when provided (line 461).'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that the undocumented 'context' field mentioned in the feature description was not present in the codebase. A grep search for 'context' in server.js returned no matches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Tool: Grep pattern: context, Result: No matches found'. Worker correctly concluded no context field removal was needed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test file follows established patterns from other contract tests: uses axios for HTTP requests, proper before/after server lifecycle management, and Node.js built-in test runner.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: context-preservation.test.js imports axios, uses before/after hooks for server management, follows structure of existing contract tests like request-shape.test.js

summary: Context preservation feature implementation passes all validation assertions. The worker took a test-driven approach, discovering that the existing server.js implementation already correctly implements stateless context preservation. All 14 new tests cover VAL-CTX-001 through VAL-CTX-005 plus edge cases for orphaned actions. Manual verification with curl confirms proper behavior: refinement requests without questionText return 400, and requests with questionText use the provided text verbatim. No code changes to server.js were needed. The implementation fully satisfies the stateless API design requirement with no session state between requests.
